response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with nearly two decades of software development, but their profile is heavily web/full-stack oriented (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Ember) rather than modern C++. Their only professional C++ experience was a short stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and there is no evidence of message-broker architectures at industrial scale in C++, MQTT/IoT protocol work, IEC 62443 or SDL secure-development practice, or industrial automation domain exposure. The systems-level and low-latency DLang work shows transferable aptitude, but the gap to the role's core requirements is too wide for a senior, mentoring-focused C++ position.
strengths: Deep systems-level experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, mentoring and workshop delivery
Broad multi-domain experience (web, mobile, desktop, embedded) with event-driven platform design (GISCollective)
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months in 2014–2015); primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No demonstrated experience with MQTT, message brokers in C++, IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial-automation/OT protocols
Recent career trajectory (full-stack product work, AI tooling) points away from embedded/industrial C++ platform development

key_factors.factor: Lack of substantial modern C++ experience
No industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / MQTT exposure
Strong low-latency systems and craftsmanship background
